rulings.Case Title: MICHEL RANTISSI VS. FRANK SHATTUCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses Without Objections, To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories (Defendant Kelly Slade)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF MICHEL RANTISSI Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses Without Objections, To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories (Defendant Kelly Slade) Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is deferred without ruling due to failure to serve copies of motion papers directly with the court's chambers. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Sets 1-2-3 And For Sanctions Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS WALKER, LARRY LOYD Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Sets 1-2-3 And For Sanctions Award Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel are directed to meet and confer in advance of the hearing (perhaps at 8:00 AM on the morning of the hearing) to determine whether the discovery issues can be narrowed. The court will defer a ruling until the hearing, at which counsel will advise the court of what progress has been made to narrow the scope of the motion. The parties are directed to bring a copy of the latest filed complaint, cross-complaint, and responses thereto to the court at the time of the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RAYNETTA RAYZETTA VS. RED AND WHITE FLEET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 28, 2012, Line 24, DEFENDANT FISHERMAN'S WHARF BAY CRUISE CORPORATION DBA RED & WHITE FLEET'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden to show that plaintiff does not meet the test for seaman status and that any negligence by defendant was not in its vessel capacity and plaintiff has not presented any admissible evidence creating a triable issue on either of these points. Defendant's evidence objections are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ZOMBIE INC. v. GREE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Zombie Inc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 7, Plaintiff Zombie Inc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are directed to meet in confer in advance of the hearing to determine whether the discovery issues in dispute can be significantly narrowed, and the parties can agree on dates for production of requested discovery items. The court will defer a ruling until the hearing. =302/JPT

Continued to October 10, 2012 to give plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK MIGDAL et al VS. MASHA LEVINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Immediate Sale Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 28, 2012, Line 29, Defendants Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's Motion For Immediate Sale Of Real Property. Granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 872.120 and 872.130 and the Court's broad equitable power in partition actions. The property located at 461 and 465 Bellevue Avenue, Oakland, California shall be sold. The proceeds from the sale shall be placed in a blocked account pending resolution of this case. It is not necessary for the Court to determine the property interests of the parties and rights of any lienholders at this time. In any event, it does not appear that the parties' respective interests in the property are disputed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAHMON MOMOH VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 28, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Defendant waited until Plaintiff was ineligible for promotion to post the open position. This shows a circumstance that suggests a discriminatory motive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 28, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. and WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the fiduciary duty cause of action and overruled as to all other causes of action. No facts are alleged that support a fiduciary relationship nor does it appear that this deficiency can be cured. The other causes of action are adequately pled.
